req.title: Create encounter
req.statement: A clinician shall create an Encounter for a patient with start and end timestamps; end before start shall be rejected.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates: (1) a clinician can successfully create an Encounter with valid start < end timestamps [positive/EP], (2) end < start must be rejected with HTTP 422 [negative/NEG], (3) end == start is a boundary condition that should be tested to clarify acceptance behaviour [boundary/BVA]. An additional Decision Table test covers a missing required field to exercise the validation matrix. State Transition Coverage is applied to verify the Encounter resource moves from non-existent to 'stored' state. Tests are self-contained via fixture injection of auth token and patient ID; each POSTs a unique payload using uuid4 to ensure idempotency.

tests.name: test_create_encounter_valid_timestamps
preconditions: Caller holds role:clinician (valid JWT in <auth_header> fixture)
Patient identified by <patient_id> fixture exists in the system
actions: POST /Encounter with resourceType, subject, start=T, end=T+1h
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains an id field
Stored period.start and period.end match submitted values
trace: R-HLT-006: encounter stored when end > start
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

def test_create_encounter_valid_timestamps(api_client, auth_header, patient_id):
    start = datetime(2024, 6, 1, 9, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    end   = start + timedelta(hours=1)
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Encounter",
        "identifier": [{"value": str(uuid.uuid4())}],
        "status": "finished",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"},
        "period": {
            "start": start.isoformat(),
            "end":   end.isoformat()
        }
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/Encounter", json=payload, headers=auth_header)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "Encounter"
    assert "id" in body
    assert body["period"]["start"] == start.isoformat()
    assert body["period"]["end"]   == end.isoformat()

tests.name: test_create_encounter_end_before_start_rejected
preconditions: Caller holds role:clinician (valid JWT in <auth_header> fixture)
Patient identified by <patient_id> fixture exists in the system
actions: POST /Encounter with period.end set 1 second before period.start
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains a diagnostic message referencing period or timestamp
trace: R-HLT-006: error pathway - 422 when end < start
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

def test_create_encounter_end_before_start_rejected(api_client, auth_header, patient_id):
    start = datetime(2024, 6, 1, 9, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    end   = start - timedelta(seconds=1)
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Encounter",
        "identifier": [{"value": str(uuid.uuid4())}],
        "status": "finished",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"},
        "period": {
            "start": start.isoformat(),
            "end":   end.isoformat()
        }
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/Encounter", json=payload, headers=auth_header)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    text = str(body).lower()
    assert "period" in text or "end" in text or "start" in text

tests.name: test_create_encounter_end_equals_start_boundary
preconditions: Caller holds role:clinician (valid JWT in <auth_header> fixture)
Patient identified by <patient_id> fixture exists in the system
actions: POST /Encounter with period.end exactly equal to period.start (zero-duration encounter)
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: HTTP 201 Created OR HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity (implementation-defined boundary)
If 201: response body contains resourceType 'Encounter' and an id
If 422: response body contains diagnostic referencing period
trace: R-HLT-006: boundary - end == start is the exact limit between valid and invalid range
BVA on period.end relative to period.start

tests.name: test_create_encounter_missing_subject_rejected
preconditions: Caller holds role:clinician (valid JWT in <auth_header> fixture)
actions: POST /Encounter with valid timestamps but omitting the required 'subject' field
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: HTTP 400 Bad Request OR HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity
Response body references 'subject' or a required-field violation
trace: R-HLT-006: precondition - patient exists; omitting subject tests validation matrix
DT: column where subject=MISSING, period=VALID => reject

tests.name: test_create_encounter_state_transition_nonexistent_to_stored
preconditions: Caller holds role:clinician (valid JWT in <auth_header> fixture)
No Encounter with the generated identifier exists (guaranteed by uuid4)
actions: POST /Encounter to transition resource from non-existent to stored state
expected: GET body resourceType equals 'Encounter' and id matches POST response
trace: R-HLT-006: encounter stored - state transition non-existent -> stored verified by retrieval
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

def test_create_encounter_state_transition_nonexistent_to_stored(
    api_client, auth_header, patient_id
):
    start = datetime(2024, 6, 2, 8, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    end   = start + timedelta(minutes=30)
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Encounter",
        "identifier": [{"value": str(uuid.uuid4())}],
        "status": "finished",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"},
        "period": {
            "start": start.isoformat(),
            "end":   end.isoformat()
        }
    }
    post_resp = api_client.post("/Encounter", json=payload, headers=auth_header)
    assert post_resp.status_code == 201
    enc_id = post_resp.json()["id"]
    get_resp = api_client.get(f"/Encounter/{enc_id}", headers=auth_header)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    body = get_resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "Encounter"
    assert body.get("id") == enc_id
